title: BlackRock Reports Second Quarter 2026 Diluted EPS | BlackRock
gist: BlackRock reported Q2 2026 results with a record $15.3 trillion in AUM, record first-half net inflows of $321 billion, 31% year-over-year revenue growth, and a raised planned quarterly share repurchase pace of $550 million.

insights.claim: BlackRock reached $15.3 trillion in assets under management following $868 billion of net inflows over the trailing twelve months, reflecting 10% organic base fee growth.
insights.speaker: BlackRock Investment Institute
insights.so_what: Double-digit organic base fee growth on a $15T-plus base signals the fee engine is compounding faster than the platform's sheer size would imply, supporting the durability of BLK's revenue growth.
insights.quote: $15.3 trillion in AUM following $868 billion of net inflows over the last twelve months, reflecting 10% organic base fee growth

insights.claim: Record first-half net inflows totaled $321 billion, including $192 billion in the second quarter, described as broad-based and driven by ETFs, private markets, active fixed income and systematic equity strategies.
insights.speaker: BlackRock Investment Institute
insights.so_what: Breadth across ETFs, private markets and active strategies means flows are not reliant on a single vehicle, reducing the risk that a rotation out of any one product category stalls organic growth.
insights.quote: Record first half net inflows of $321 billion including $192 billion in the second quarter, broad-based across the platform

insights.claim: Revenue rose 31% year-over-year, attributed to positive market impact, organic base fee growth, fees related to the HPS Transaction, higher performance fees, and higher technology services and subscription revenue.
insights.speaker: BlackRock Investment Institute
insights.so_what: The HPS Transaction fees and performance fees add inorganic and variable components to the 31% print, so investors should separate durable base-fee growth from one-off and market-sensitive contributions when modeling forward revenue.
insights.quote: 31% increase in revenue year-over-year reflects the positive impact of markets, organic base fee growth, fees related to the HPS Transaction, higher performance fees

insights.claim: Technology services and subscription revenue grew 13% year-over-year, driven by continued momentum in Aladdin and multi-product solutions.
insights.speaker: BlackRock Investment Institute
insights.so_what: Recurring, subscription-based Aladdin revenue offers a higher-multiple, less market-correlated income stream that can re-rate BlackRock away from a pure asset-manager valuation.
insights.quote: 13% growth in technology services and subscription revenue year over-year, driven by continued momentum in Aladdin

insights.claim: Operating income increased 42% year-over-year (39% as adjusted) while diluted EPS rose 20% (15% as adjusted), with EPS growth held back by lower nonoperating income and a higher diluted share count.
insights.speaker: BlackRock Investment Institute
insights.so_what: The gap between 42% operating income growth and 20% EPS growth flags share-count dilution and weaker nonoperating income as drags, so operating leverage is stronger than the headline EPS suggests.
insights.quote: 20% increase in diluted EPS year-over-year (15% as adjusted) also reflects a lower nonoperating income and higher diluted share count in the current quarter

insights.claim: BlackRock repurchased $450 million of shares in the quarter and is increasing its planned quarterly share repurchases to $550 million.
insights.speaker: BlackRock Investment Institute
insights.so_what: Raising the buyback pace signals management confidence in cash generation and can partly offset the dilution that pressured EPS this quarter.
insights.quote: Increasing planned quarterly share repurchases to $550 million
